verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Who hath believed our report? and to whom is the arm of the Lord revealed?…He is brought as a lamb to the slaughter, &c.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Every deed of Christ is a cause of glorying to the Catholic Church, but her greatest of all glorying is in the Cross; and knowing this, Paul says, But God forbid that I should glory, save in the Cross of Christ.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For wondrous indeed it was, that one who was blind from his birth should receive sight in Siloam; but what is this compared with the blind of the whole world?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: A great thing it was, and passing nature, for Lazarus to rise again on the fourth day; but the grace extended to him alone, and what was it compared with the dead in sins throughout the world?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Marvellous it was, that five loaves should pour forth food for the five thousand; but what is that to those who are famishing in ignorance through all the world?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It was marvellous that she should have been loosed who had been bound by Satan eighteen years: yet what is this to all of us, who were fast bound in the chains of our sins?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the glory of the Cross led those who were blind through ignorance into light, loosed all who were held fast by sin, and ransomed the whole world of mankind.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wonder not that the whole world was ransomed; for it was no mere man, but the only-begotten Son of God, who died on its behalf.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Moreover one man’s sin, even Adam’s, had power to bring death to the world; but if by the trespass of the one death reigned over the world, how shall not life much rather reign by the righteousness of the One?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if because of the tree of food they were then cast out of paradise, shall not believers now more easily enter into paradise because of the Tree of Jesus?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If the first man formed out of the earth brought in universal death, shall not He who formed him out of the earth bring in eternal life, being Himself the Life?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If Phinees, when he waxed zealous and slew the evil-doer, staved the wrath of God, shall not Jesus, who slew not another, but gave up Himself for a ransom, put away the wrath which is against mankind?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us then not be ashamed of the Cross of our Saviour, but rather glory in it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the word of the Cross is unto Jews a stumbling-block, and unto Gentiles foolishness, but to us salvation: and to them that are perishing it is foolishness, but unto us which are being saved it is the power of God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it was not a mere man who died for us, as I said before, but the Son of God, God made man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Further; if the lamb under Moses drove the destroyer far away, did not much rather the Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world, deliver us from our sins?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The blood of a silly sheep gave salvation; and shall not the Blood of the Only-begotten much rather save?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If any disbelieve the power of the Crucified, let him ask the devils; if any believe not words, let him believe what he sees.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Many have been crucified throughout the world, but by none of these are the devils scared; but when they see even the Sign of the Cross of Christ, who was crucified for us, they shudder.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For those men died for their own sins, but Christ for the sins of others; for He did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is not Peter who says this, for then we might suspect that he was partial to his Teacher; but it is Esaias who says it, who was not indeed present with Him in the flesh, but in the Spirit foresaw His coming in the flesh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yet why now bring the Prophet only as a witness? take for a witness Pilate himself, who gave sentence upon Him, saying, I find no fault in this Man: and when he gave Him up, and had washed his hands, he said, I am innocent of the blood of this just person.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There is yet another witness of the sinlessness of Jesus,—the robber, the first man admitted into Paradise; who rebuked his fellow, and said, “We receive the due reward of our deeds; but this man hath done nothing amiss; for we were present, both thou and I, at His judgment.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jesus then really suffered for all men; for the Cross was no illusion, otherwise our redemption is an illusion also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His death was not a mere show, for then is our salvation also fabulous.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If His death was but a show, they were true who said, We remember that that deceiver said, while He was yet alive, After three days I rise again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: His Passion then was real: for He was really crucified, and we are not ashamed thereat; He was crucified, and we deny it not, nay, I rather glory to speak of it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For though I should now deny it, here is Golgotha to confute me, near which we are now assembled; the wood of the Cross confutes me, which was afterwards distributed piecemeal from hence to all the world.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I confess the Cross, because I know of the Resurrection; for if, after being crucified, He had remained as He was, I had not perchance confessed it, for I might have concealed both it and my Master; but now that the Resurrection has followed the Cross, I am not ashamed to declare it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Being then in the flesh like others, He was crucified, but not for the like sins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For He was not led to death for covetousness, since He was a Teacher of poverty; nor was He condemned for concupiscence, for He Himself says plainly, Whosoever shall look upon a woman to lust after her, hath committed adultery with her already; not for smiting or striking hastily, for He turned the other cheek also to the smiter; not for despising the Law, for He was the fulfiller of the Law; not for reviling a prophet, for it was Himself who was proclaimed by the Prophets; not for defrauding any of their hire, for He ministered without reward and freely; not for sinning in words, or deeds, or thoughts, He who did no sin, neither was guile found in His mouth; who when He was reviled, reviled not again; when He suffered, threatened not; who came to His passion, not unwillingly, but willing; yea, if any dissuading Him say even now, Be it far from Thee, Lord, He will say again, Get thee behind Me, Satan.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou be persuaded that He came to His passion willingly? others, who foreknow it not, die unwillingly; but He spoke before of His passion: Behold, the Son of man is betrayed to be crucified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But knowest thou wherefore this Friend of man shunned not death?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It was lest the whole world should perish in its sins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man shall be betrayed, and shall be crucified; and again, He stedfastly set His face to go to Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou know certainly, that the Cross is a glory to Jesus?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear His own words, not mine.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Judas had become ungrateful to the Master of the house, and was about to betray Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Having but just now gone forth from the table, and drunk His cup of blessing, in return for that drought of salvation he sought to shed righteous blood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He who did eat of His bread, was lifting up his heel against Him; his hands were but lately receiving the blessed gifts, and presently for the wages of betrayal he was plotting His death.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And being reproved, and having heard that word, Thou hast said, he again went out: then said Jesus, The hour is come, that the Son of man should be glorified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou how He knew the Cross to be His proper glory?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then, is Esaias not ashamed of being sawn asunder, and shall Christ be ashamed of dying for the world?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now is the Son of man glorified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Not that He was without glory before: for He was glorified with the glory which was before the foundation of the world.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He was ever glorified as God; but now He was to be glorified in wearing the Crown of His patience.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He gave not up His life by compulsion, nor was He put to death by murderous violence, but of His own accord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear what He says: I have power to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again: I yield it of My own choice to My enemies; for unless I chose, this could not be.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He came therefore of His own set purpose to His passion, rejoicing in His noble deed, smiling at the crown, cheered by the salvation of mankind; not ashamed of the Cross, for it was to save the world.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it was no common man who suffered, but God in man’s nature, striving for the prize of His patience.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the Jews contradict this, ever ready, as they are, to cavil, and backward to believe; so that for this cause the Prophet just now read says, Lord, who hath believed our report?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Persians believe, and Hebrews believe not; they shall see, to whom He was not spoken of, and they that have not heard shall understand, while they who study these things shall set at nought what they study.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They speak against us, and say, “Does the Lord then suffer?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Had men’s hands power over His sovereignty?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Read the Lamentations; for in those Lamentations, Jeremias, lamenting you, wrote what is worthy of lamentations.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He saw your destruction, he beheld your downfall, he bewailed Jerusalem which then was; for that which now is shall not be bewailed; for that Jerusalem crucified the Christ, but that which now is worships Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Lamenting then he says, The breath of our countenance, Christ the Lord was taken in our corruptions.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Am I then stating views of my own?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Behold he testifies of the Lord Christ seized by men.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what is to follow from this?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell me, O Prophet.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He says, Of whom we said, Under His shadow we shall live among the nations.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he signifies that the grace of life is no longer to dwell in Israel, but among the Gentiles.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But since there has been much gainsaying by them, come, let me, with the help of your prayers, (as the shortness of the time may allow,) set forth by the grace of the Lord some few testimonies concerning the Passion.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the things concerning Christ are all put into writing, and nothing is doubtful, for nothing is without a text.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: All are inscribed on the monuments of the Prophets; clearly written, not on tablets of stone, but by the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Since then thou hast heard the Gospel speaking concerning Judas, oughtest thou not to receive the testimony to it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He was pierced in the side by a spear; oughtest thou not to see whether this also is written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He was crucified in a garden; oughtest thou not to see whether this also is written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He was sold for thirty pieces of silver; oughtest thou not to learn what prophet spake this?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He was given vinegar to drink; learn where this also is written.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that His body was laid in a rock, and that a stone was set over it; oughtest thou not to receive this testimony also from the prophet?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He was crucified with robbers; oughtest thou not to see whether this also is written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He was buried; oughtest thou not to see whether the circumstances of His burial are anywhere accurately written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast heard that He rose again; oughtest thou not to see whether we mock thee in teaching these things?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For our speech and our preaching is not in persuasive words of man’s wisdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: We stir now no sophistical contrivances; for these become exposed; we do not conquer words with words, for these come to an end; but we preach Christ Crucified, who has already been preached aforetime by the Prophets.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But do thou, I pray, receive the testimonies, and seal them in thine heart.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And, since they are many, and the rest of our time is narrowed into a short space, listen now to a few of the more important as time permits; and having received these beginnings, be diligent and seek out the remainder.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let not thine hand be only stretched out to receive, but let it be also ready to work.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: God gives all things freely.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For if any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God who giveth, and he shall receive.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: May He through your prayer grant utterance to us who speak, and faith to you who hear.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us then seek the testimonies to the Passion of Christ: for we are met together, not now to make a speculative exposition of the Scriptures, but rather to be certified of the things which we already believe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now thou hast received from me first the testimonies concerning the coming of Jesus; and concerning His walking on the sea, for it is written, Thy way is in the sea.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Also concerning divers cures thou hast on another occasion received testimony.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now therefore I begin from whence the Passion began.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Judas was the traitor, and he came against Him, and stood, speaking words of peace, but plotting war.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Concerning him, therefore, the Psalmist says, My friends and My neighbours drew near against Me, and stood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, Their words were softer than oil, yet be they spears.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hail, Master; yet he was betraying his Master to death; he was not abashed at His warning, when He said, Judas, betrayest than the Son of Man with a kiss? for what He said to him was just this, Recollect thine own name; Judas means confession; thou hast covenanted, thou hast received the money, make confession quickly.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O God, pass not over My praise in silence; for the mouth of the wicked, and the mouth of the deceitful, are opened against Me; they have spoken against Me with a treacherous tongue, they have compassed Me about also with words of hatred.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But that some of the chief-priests also were present, and that He was put in bonds before the gates of the city, thou hast heard before, if thou rememberest the exposition of the Psalm, which has told the time and the place; how they returned at evening, and hungered like dogs, and encompassed the city.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Listen also for the thirty pieces of silver.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And I will say to them, If it be good in your sight, give me my price, or refuse, and the rest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: One price is owing to Me from you for My healing the blind and lame, and I receive another; for thanksgiving, dishonour, and for worship, insult.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou how the Scripture foresaw these things?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And they weighed for My price thirty pieces of silver.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: How exact the prophecy! how great and unerring the wisdom of the Holy Ghost!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For he said, not ten, nor twenty, but thirty, exactly as many as there were.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell also what becomes of this price, O Prophet!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does he who received it keep it? or does he give it back? and after he has given it back, what becomes of it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Prophet says then, And I took the thirty pieces of silver, and cast them into the house of the Lord, into the foundry.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Compare the Gospel with the Prophecy: Judas, it says, repented himself, and cast down the pieces of silver in the temple, and departed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But now I have to seek the exact solution of this seeming discrepancy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For they who make light of the prophets, allege that the Prophet says on the one hand, And I cast them into the house of the Lord, into the foundry, but the Gospel on the other hand, And they gave them for the potter’s field.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear then how they are both true.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For those conscientious Jews forsooth, the high-priests of that time, when they saw that Judas repented and said, I have sinned, in that I have betrayed innocent blood, reply, What is that to us, see thou to that.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it then nothing to you, the crucifiers? but shall he who received and restored the price of murder see to it, and shall ye the murderers not see to it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Then they say among themselves, It is not lawful to cast them into the treasury, because it is the price of blood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Out of your own mouths is your condemnation; if the price is polluted, the deed is polluted also: but if thou art fulfilling righteousness in crucifying Christ, why receivest thou not the price of it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the point of iniquity is this: how is there no disagreement, if the Gospel says, the potter’s field, and the Prophet, the foundry?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay, but not only people who are goldsmiths, or brass-founders, have a foundry, but potters also have foundries for their clay.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For they sift off the fine and rich and useful earth from the gravel, and separate from it the mass of the refuse matter, and temper the clay first with water, that they may work it with ease into the forms intended.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Why then wonderest thou that the Gospel says plainly the potter’s field, whereas the Prophet spoke his prophecy like an enigma, since prophecy is in many places enigmatical?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They bound Jesus, and brought Him into the hall of the High-priest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou learn and know that this also is written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Esaias says, Woe unto their soul, for they have taken evil counsel against themselves, saying, Let us bind the Just, for He is troublesome to us.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And truly, Woe unto their soul!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us see how Esaias was sawn asunder, yet after this the people was restored.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jeremias was cast into the mire of the cistern, yet was the wound of the Jews healed; for the sin was less, since it was against man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But when the Jews sinned, not against man, but against God in man’s nature, Woe unto their soul!—Let us bind the Just; could He not then set Himself free, some one will say; He, who freed Lazarus from the bonds of death on the fourth day, and loosed Peter from the iron bands of a prison?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Angels stood ready at hand, saying, Let us burst their bands in sunder; but they hold back, because their Lord willed to undergo it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again, He was led to the judgment-seat before the Elders; thou hast already the testimony to this, The Lord Himself will come into judgment with the ancients of His people, and with the princes thereof.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the High-priest having questioned Him, and heard the truth, is wroth; and the wicked officer of wicked men smites Him; and the countenance, which had shone as the sun, endured to be smitten by lawless hands.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Others also come and spit on the face of Him, who by spittle had healed the man who was blind from his birth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Do ye thus requite the Lord?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This people is foolish and unwise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the Prophet greatly wondering, says, Lord, who hath believed our report? for the thing is incredible, that God, the Son of God, and the Arm of the Lord, should suffer such things.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But that they who are being saved may not disbelieve, the Holy Ghost writes before, in the person of Christ, who says, (for He who then spake these things, was afterward Himself an actor in them,) I gave My back to the scourges; (for Pilate, when he had scourged Him, delivered Him to be crucified;) and My cheeks to smitings; and My face I turned not away from the shame of spittings; saying, as it were, “Though knowing before that they will smite Me, I did not even turn My cheek aside; for how should I have nerved My disciples against death for truth’s sake, had I Myself dreaded this?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He that loveth his life shall lose it: if I had loved My life, how was I to teach without practising what I taught?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: First then, being Himself God, He endured to suffer these things at the hands of men; that after this, we men, when we suffer such things at the hands of men for His sake, might not be ashamed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest that of these things also the prophets have clearly written beforehand.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Many, however, of the Scripture testimonies I pass by for want of time, as I said before; for if one should exactly search out all, not one of the things concerning Christ would be left without witness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Having been bound, He came from Caiaphas to Pilate,—is this too written? yes; And having bound Him, they led Him away as a present to the king of Jarim.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But here some sharp hearer will object, “Pilate was not a king,” (to leave for a while the main parts of the question,) “how then having bound Him, led they Him as a present to the king?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But read thou the Gospel; When Pilate heard that He was of Galilee, he sent Him to Herod; for Herod was then king, and was present at Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And now observe the exactness of the Prophet; for he says, that He was sent as a present; for the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together, for before they were at enmity.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it became Him who was on the eve of making peace between earth and heaven, to make the very men who condemned Him the first to be at peace; for the Lord Himself was there present, who reconciles the hearts of the princes of the earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Mark the exactness of the Prophets, and their true testimony.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Look with awe then at the Lord who was judged.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He suffered Himself to be led and carried by soldiers.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Pilate sat in judgment, and He who sitteth on the right hand of the Father, stood and was judged.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The people whom He had redeemed from the land of Egypt, and oftimes from other places, shouted against Him, Away with Him, away with Him, crucify Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wherefore, O ye Jews? because He healed your blind? or because He made your lame to walk, and bestowed His other benefits?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: So that the Prophet in amazement speaks of this too, Against whom have ye opened your mouth, and against whom have ye let loose your tongue? and the Lord Himself says in the Prophets, Mine heritage became unto Me as a lion in the forest; it gave its voice against Me; therefore have I hated it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I have not refused them, but they have refused Me; in consequence thereof I say, I have forsaken My house.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When He was judged, He held His peace; so that Pilate was moved for Him, and said, Hearest Thou not what these witness against Thee?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Not that He knew Him who was judged, but he feared his own wife’s dream which had been reported to him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And Jesus held His peace.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Psalmist says, And I became as a man that heareth not; and in whose mouth are no reproofs; and again, But I was as a deaf man and heard not; and as a dumb man that openeth not his mouth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast before heard concerning this, if thou rememberest.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the soldiers who crowd around mock Him, and their Lord becomes a sport to them, and upon their Master they make jests.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When they looked on Me, they shaked their heads.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yet the figure of kingly state appears; for though in mockery, yet they bend the knee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the soldiers before they crucify Him, put on Him a purple robe, and set a crown on His head; for what though it be of thorns?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Every king is proclaimed by soldiers; and Jesus also must in a figure be crowned by soldiers; so that for this cause the Scripture says in the Canticles, Go forth, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, and look upon King Solomon in the crown wherewith His mother crowned Him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And the crown itself was a mystery; for it was a remission of sins, a release from the curse.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Adam received the sentence, Cursed is the ground in thy labours; thorns and thistles shall it bring forth to thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For this cause Jesus assumes the thorns, that He may cancel the sentence; for this cause also was He buried in the earth, that the earth which had been cursed might receive the blessing instead of a curse.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: At the time of the sin, they clothed themselves with fig-leaves; for this cause Jesus also made the fig-tree the last of His signs.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For when about to go to His passion, He curses the fig-tree, not every fig-tree, but that one alone, for the sake of the figure; saying, No more let any man eat fruit of thee; let the doom be cancelled.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And because they aforetime clothed themselves with fig-leaves, He came at a season when food was not wont to be found on the fig-tree.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Who knows not that in winter-time the fig-tree bears no fruit, but is clothed with leaves only?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Was Jesus ignorant of this, which all knew?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: No, but though He knew, yet He came as if seeking; not ignorant that He should not find, but shewing that the emblematical curse extended to the leaves only.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And since we have touched on things connected with Paradise, I am truly astonished at the truth of the types.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In Paradise was the Fall, and in a Garden was our Salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: From the Tree came sin, and until the Tree sin lasted.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In the evening, when the Lord walked in the Garden, they hid themselves; and in the evening the robber is brought by the Lord into Paradise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But some one will say to me, “Thou art inventing subtleties; shew me from some prophet the Wood of the Cross; except thou give me a testimony from a prophet, I will not be persuaded.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Hear then from Jeremias, and assure thyself; I was like a harmless lamb led to be slaughtered; did I not know it? (for in this manner read it as a question, as I have read it; for He who said, Ye know that after two days comes the passover, and the Son of Man is betrayed to be crucified, did He not know?)

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I was like a harmless lamb led to be slaughtered; did I not know it? (but what sort of lamb? let John the Baptist interpret it, when he says, Behold the Lamb of God, that taketh away the sin of the world.)

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They devised against Me a wicked device, saying—(He who knows the devices, knew He not the result of them?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what said they?)—Come, and let us place a beam upon His bread—(and if the Lord reckon thee worthy, thou shalt hereafter learn, that His body according to the Gospel bore the figure of bread;)—Come then, and let us place a beam upon His bread, and cut Him off out of the land of the living;—(life is not cut off, why labour ye for nought?)—And His name shall be remembered no more.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Vain is your counsel; for before the sun His Name abideth in the Church.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And that it was Life, which hung on the Cross, Moses says, weeping, And thy life shall be hanging before thine eyes; and thou shalt be afraid day and night, and thou shalt not trust thy life.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And so too, what was just now read as the text, Lord, who hath believed our report?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This was the figure which Moses completed by fixing the serpent to a cross, that whoso had been bitten by the living serpent, and looked to the brasen serpent, might be saved by believing.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Does then the brazen serpent save when crucified, and shall not the Son of God incarnate save when crucified also?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: On each occasion life comes by means of wood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For in the time of Noe the preservation of life was by an ark of wood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: In the time of Moses the sea, on beholding the emblematical rod, was abashed at him who smote it; is then Moses’ rod mighty, and is the Cross of the Saviour powerless?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But I pass by the greater part of the types, to keep within measure.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The wood in Moses’ case sweetened the water; and from the side of Jesus the water flowed upon the wood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The beginning of signs under Moses was blood and water; and the last of all Jesus’ signs was the same.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: First, Moses changed the river into blood; and Jesus at the last gave forth from His side water with blood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This was perhaps on account of the two speeches, his who judged Him, and theirs who cried out against Him; or because of the believers and the unbelievers.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Pilate said, I am innocent and washed his hands in water; they who cried out against Him said, His blood be upon us: there came therefore these two out of His side; the water, perhaps, for him who judged Him; but for them that shouted against Him the blood.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again it is to be understood in another way; the blood for the Jews, and the water for the Christians: for upon them as plotters came the condemnation from the blood; but to thee who now believest, the salvation which is by water.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For nothing has been done without a meaning.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Our fathers who have written comments have given another reason of this matter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For since in the Gospels the power of salutary Baptism is twofold, one which is granted by means of water to the illuminated, and a second to holy martyrs, in persecutions, through their own blood, there came out of that saving Side blood and water, to confirm the grace of the confession made for Christ, whether in baptism, or on occasions of martyrdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There is another reason also for mentioning the Side.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The woman, who was formed from the side, led the way to sin; but Jesus who came to bestow the grace of pardon on men and women alike, was pierced in the side for women, that He might undo the sin.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And whoever will inquire, will find other reasons also; but what has been said is enough, because of the shortness of the time, and that the attention of my hearers may not become sated.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And yet we never can be tired of hearing concerning the crowning of our Lord, and least of all in this most holy Golgotha.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For others only hear, but we both see and handle.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let none be weary; take thine armour against the adversaries in the cause of the Cross itself; set up the faith of the Cross as a trophy against the gainsayers.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For when thou art going to dispute with unbelievers concerning the Cross of Christ, first make with thy hand the sign of Christ’s Cross, and the gainsayer will be silenced.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be not ashamed to confess the Cross; for Angels glory in it, saying, We know whom ye seek, Jesus the Crucified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Mightest thou not say, O Angel, “I know whom ye seek, my Master?”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But, “I,” he says with boldness, “I know the Crucified.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the Cross is a Crown, not a dishonour.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now let us recur to the proof out of the Prophets which I spoke of.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Lord was crucified; thou hast received the testimonies.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest this spot of Golgotha!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou answerest with a shout of praise, as if assenting.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: See that thou recant not in time of persecution.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Rejoice not in the Cross in time of peace only, but hold fast the same faith in time of persecution also; be not in time of peace a friend of Jesus, and His foe in time of wars.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou receivest now remission of thy sins, and the gifts of the King’s spiritual bounty; when war shall come, strive thou nobly for thy King.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jesus, the Sinless, was crucified for thee; and wilt not thou be crucified for Him who was crucified for thee?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou art not bestowing a favour, for thou hast first received; but thou art returning a favour, repaying thy debt to Him who was crucified for thee in Golgotha.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now Golgotha is interpreted, “the place of a skull.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Who were they then, who prophetically named this spot Golgotha, in which Christ the true Head endured the Cross?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: As the Apostle says, Who is the Image of the Invisible God; and a little after, and He is the Head of the body, the Church.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, The Head of every man is Christ; and again, Who is the Head of all principality and power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Head suffered in “the place of the skull.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O wondrous prophetic appellation!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The very name also reminds thee, saying, “Think not of the Crucified as of a mere man; He is the Head of all principality and power.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That Head which was crucified is the Head of all power, and has for His Head the Father; for the Head of the man is Christ, and the Head of Christ is God.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Christ then was crucified for us, who was judged in the night, when it was cold, and therefore a fire of coals was laid.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He was crucified at the third hour; and from the sixth hour there was darkness until the ninth hour; but from the ninth hour there was light again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Are these things also written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us inquire.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now the Prophet Zacharias says, And it shall come to pass in that day, that there shall not be light, and there shall be cold and frost one day; (the cold on account of which Peter warmed himself;) And that day shall be known unto the Lord; (what, knew He not the other days? days are many, but this is the day of the Lord’s patience, which the Lord made;)—And that day shall be known unto the Lord, not day, and not night: what is this dark saying which the Prophet speaks?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: That day is neither day nor night? what then shall we name it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Gospel interprets it, by relating the event.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It was not day; for the sun shone not uniformly from his rising to his setting, but from the sixth hour till the ninth hour, there was darkness at mid-day.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The darkness therefore was interposed; but God called the darkness night.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wherefore it was neither day nor night: for neither was it all light, that it should be called day; nor was it all darkness, that it should be called night; but after the ninth hour the sun shone forth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This also the Prophet foretels; for after saying, Not day, nor night, he added, And at evening time it shall be light.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou the exactness of the prophets?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Seest thou the truth of the things which were written aforetime?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But dost thou ask exactly at what hour the sun failed? was it the fifth hour, or the eighth, or the tenth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell, O Prophet, the exact time thereof to the Jews, who are unwilling to hear; when shall the sun go down?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Prophet Amos answers, And it shall come to pass in that day, saith the Lord God, that the sun shall go down at noon (for there was darkness from the sixth hour;) and the light shall grow dark over the earth in the day.”

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What sort of season is this, O Prophet, and what sort of day?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And I will turn your feasts into mourning; for this was done in the days of unleavened bread, and at the feast of the Passover: then afterwards he says, And I will make Him as the mourning of an Only Son, and those with Him as a day of anguish; for in the day of unleavened bread, and at the feast, their women were wailing and weeping, and the Apostles had hidden themselves and were in anguish.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Wonderful then is this prophecy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But, some one will say, “Give me yet another sign; what other exact sign is there of that which has come to pass?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jesus was crucified; and He wore but one coat, and one cloak: now His cloak the soldiers shared among themselves, having rent it into four; but His coat was not rent, for when rent it would have been no longer of any use; so about this lots are cast by the soldiers; thus the one they divide, but for the other they cast lots.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is then this also written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They know, the diligent chanters of the Church, who imitate the Angel hosts, and continually sing praises to God: who are thought worthy to chant Psalms in this Golgotha, and to say, They parted My garments among them, and upon My vesture they did cast lots.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The “lots” were what the soldiers cast.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again, when He had been judged before Pilate, He was clothed in red; for there they put on Him a purple robe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is this also written?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Esaias saith, Who is this that cometh from Edom? the redness of His garments is from Bosor; (who is this who in dishonor weareth purple?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Bosor has some such meaning in Hebrew.)

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Why are Thy garments red, and Thy raiment as from a trodden wine-press?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But He answers and says, All day long have I stretched forth Mine hands unto a disobedient and gainsaying people.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He stretched out His hands on the Cross, that He might embrace the ends of the world; for this Golgotha is the very centre of the earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is not my word, but it is a prophet who hath said, Thou hast wrought salvation in the midst of the earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He stretched forth human hands, who by His spiritual hands had established the heaven; and they were fastened with nails, that His manhood, which here the sins of men, having been nailed to the tree, and having died, sin might die with it, and we might rise again in righteousness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For since by one man came death, by One Man came also life; by One Man, the Saviour, dying of His own accord: for remember what He said, I have power to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But though He endured these things, having come for the salvation of all, yet the people returned Him an evil recompense.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Jesus saith, I thirst,—He who had brought forth the waters for them out of the craggy rock; and He asked fruit of the Vine which He had planted.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But what does the Vine?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This Vine, which was by nature of the holy fathers, but of Sodom by purpose of heart;—(for their Vine is of Sodom, and their tendrils of Gomorrah;)—this Vine, when the Lord was athirst, having filled a sponge and put it on a reed, offers Him vinegar.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They gave Me also gall for My meat, and in My thirst, they gave Me vinegar to drink.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou seest the clearness of the Prophets’ description.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But what sort of gall put they into My mouth?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They gave Him, it says, wine mingled with myrrh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Now myrrh is in taste like gall, and very bitter.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Are these things what ye recompense unto the Lord?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Are these thy offerings, O Vine, unto thy Master?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Rightly did the Prophet Esaias aforetime bewail you, saying, My well-beloved had a vineyard in a hill in a fruitful place; and (not to recite the whole) I waited, he says, that it should bring forth grapes; I thirsted that it should give wine; but it brought forth thorns; for thou seest the crown, wherewith I am adorned.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What then shall I now decree?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I will command the clouds that they rain no rain upon it.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the clouds which are the Prophets were removed from them, and are for the future in the Church; as Paul says, Let the Prophets speak two or three, and let the others judge; and again, God gave in the Church, some, Apostles, and some, Prophets.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Agabus, who bound his own feet and hands, was a prophet.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Concerning the robbers who were crucified with Him, it is written, And He was numbered with the transgressors.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Both of them were before this transgressors, but one was so no longer.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the one was a transgressor to the end, stubborn against salvation; who, though his hands were fastened, smote with blasphemy by his tongue.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: When the Jews passing by wagged their heads, mocking the Crucified, and fulfilling what was written, When they looked on Me, they shaked their heads, he also reviled with them.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But the other rebused the reviler; and it was to him the end of life and the beginning of restoration; the surrender of his soul a first share in salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And after rebuking the other, he says, Lord, remember me; for with Thee is my account.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Heed not this man, for the eyes of his understanding are blinded; but remember me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I say not, remember my works, for of these I am afraid.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Every man has a feeling for his fellow-traveller; I am travelling with Thee towards death; remember me, Thy fellow-wayfarer.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I say not, Remember me now, but, when Thou comest in Thy kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What power, O robber, led thee to the light?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Who taught thee to worship that despised Man, thy companion on the Cross?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O Light Eternal, which gives light to them that are in darkness!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore also he justly heard the words, Be of good cheer; not that thy deeds are worthy of good cheer; but that the King is here, dispensing favours.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The request reached unto a distant time; but the grace was very speedy.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Verily I say unto thee, This day shalt thou be with Me in Paradise; because to-day thou hast heard My voice, and hast not hardened thine heart.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Very speedily I passed sentence upon Adam, very speedily I pardon thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: To him it was said, In the day wherein ye eat, ye shall surely die; but thou to-day hast obeyed the faith, to-day is thy salvation.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Adam by the Tree fell away; thou by the Tree art brought into Paradise.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Fear not the serpent; he shall not cast thee out; for he is fallen from heaven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And I say not unto thee, This day shalt thou depart, but, This day shalt thou be with Me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be of good courage: thou shalt not be cast out.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Fear not the flaming sword; it shrinks from its Lord.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: O mighty and ineffable grace!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The faithful Abraham had not yet entered, but the robber enters!

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Moses and the Prophets had not yet entered, and the robber enters though a breaker of the law.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Paul also wondered at this before thee, saying, Where sin abounded, there grace did much more abound.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They who had borne the heat of the day had not yet entered; and he of the eleventh hour entered.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let none murmur against the goodman of the house, for he says, Friend, I do thee no wrong; is it not lawful for Me to do what I will with Mine own?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The robber has a will to work righteousness, but death prevents him; I wait not exclusively for the work, but faith also I accept.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I am come who feed My sheep among the lilies, I am come to feed them in the gardens.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I have found a sheep that was lost, but I lay it on My shoulders; for he believes, since he himself has said, I have gone astray like a lost sheep; Lord, remember me when Thou comest in Thy kingdom.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of this garden I sang of old to My spouse in the Canticles, and spoke to her thus.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I am come into My garden, My sister, My spouse; (now in the place where He was crucified was a garden;) and what takest Thou thence?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I have gathered My myrrh; having drunk wine mingled with myrrh, and vinegar, after receiving which, He said, It is finished.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For the mystery has been fulfilled; the things that are written have been accomplished; sins are forgiven.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For Christ being come an High-Priest of the good things to come, by the greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this creation, nor yet by the blood of goats and calves, but by His own blood, entered in once for all into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption; for if the blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer, sprinkling the defiled, sanctifieth to the purifying of the flesh, how much more the blood of Christ?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again, Having therefore, brethren, boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus, by a new and living way, which He hath consecrated for us, through the veil, that is to say, His flesh.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And because His flesh, this veil, was dishonoured, therefore the typical veil of the temple was rent asunder, as it is written, And, behold, the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom; for not a particle of it was left; for since the Master said, Behold, your house is left unto you desolate, the house brake all in pieces.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: These things the Saviour endured, and made peace through the Blood of His Cross, for things in heaven, and things in earth.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For we were enemies of God through sin, and God had appointed the sinner to die.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: There must needs therefore have happened one of two things; either that God, in His truth, should destroy all men, or that in His loving-kindness He should cancel the sentence.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But behold the wisdom of God; He preserved both the truth of His sentence, and the exercise of His loving-kindness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Christ took our sins in His body on the tree, that we by His death might die to sin, and live unto righteousness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Of no small account was He who died for us; He was not a literal sheep; He was not a mere man; He was more than an Angel; He was God made man.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The transgression of sinners was not so great as the righteousness of Him who died for them; the sin which we committed was not so great as the righteousness which He wrought who laid down His life for us,—who laid it down when He pleased, and took it again when He pleased.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And wouldest thou know that He laid not down His life by violence, nor yielded up the ghost against His will?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: He cried to the Father, saying, Father, into Thy hands I commend My spirit; I commend it, that I may take it again.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And having said these things, He gave up the ghost; but not for any long time, for He quickly rose again from the dead.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Sun was darkened, because of the Sun of Righteousness.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Rocks were rent, because of the spiritual Rock.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tombs were opened, and the dead arose, because of Him who was free among the dead; He sent forth His prisoners out of the pit wherein is no water.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be not then ashamed of the Crucified, but be thou also bold to say, He beareth our sins, and endureth grief for us, and with His stripes we are healed.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not be unthankful to our Benefactor.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And again; for the transgression of my people was He led to death; and I will give the wicked for His burial, and the rich for His death.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Therefore Paul says plainly, that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures, and that He was buried, and that He hath risen again the third day according to the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: But we seek to know clearly where He has been buried.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is His tomb made with hands?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is it, like the tombs of kings, raised above the ground?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Is the Sepulchre made of stones joined together?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what is laid upon it?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Tell us, O Prophets, the exact truth concerning His tomb also, where He is laid, and where we shall seek Him?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And they say, Look into the solid rock which ye have hewn.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Look in and behold.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast in the Gospels In a sepulchre hewn in stone, which was hewn out of a rock.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And what happens next?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What kind of door has the sepulchre?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Again another Prophet says, They cut off My life in a dungeon, and cast a stone upon Me.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I, who am the Chief corner-stone, the elect, the precious, lie for a little time within a stone—I who am a stone of stumbling to the Jews, and of salvation to them who believe.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The Tree of life, therefore was planted in the earth, that the earth which had been cursed might enjoy the blessing, and that the dead might be released.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let us not then be ashamed to confess the Crucified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Be the Cross our seal made with boldness by our fingers on our brow, and on everything; over the bread we eat, and the cups we drink; in our comings in, and goings out; before our sleep, when we lie down and when we rise up; when we are in the way, and when we are still.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Great is that preservative; it is without price, for the sake of the poor; without toil, for the sick; since also its grace is from God.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It is the Sign of the faithful, and the dread of devils: for He triumphed over them in it, having made a shew of them openly; for when they see the Cross they are reminded of the Crucified; they are afraid of Him, who bruised the heads of the dragon.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Despise not the Seal, because of the freeness of the gift; out for this the rather honour thy Benefactor.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And if thou ever fall into disputation and hast not the grounds of proof, yet let Faith remain firm in thee; or rather, become thou well learned, and then silence the Jews out of the prophets, and the Greeks out of their own fables.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: They themselves worship men who have been thunderstricken: but the thunder when it comes from heaven, comes not at random.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If they are not ashamed to worship men thunderstricken and abhorred of God, art thou ashamed to worship the beloved Son of God, who was crucified for thee?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I am ashamed to tell the tales about their so-called Gods, and I leave them because of time; let those who know, speak.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: And let all heretics also be silenced.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If any say that the Cross is an illusion, turn away from him.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Abhor those who say that Christ was crucified to our fancy only; for if so, and if salvation is from the Cross, then is salvation a fancy also.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If the Cross is fancy, the Resurrection is fancy also; but if Christ be not risen, we are yet in our sins.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: If the Cross is fancy, the Ascension also is fancy; and if the Ascension is fancy, then is the second coming also fancy, and everything is henceforth unsubstantial.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Take therefore first, as an indestructible foundation, the Cross, and build upon it the other articles of the faith.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Deny not the Crucified; for, if thou deny Him, thou hast many to arraign thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Judas the traitor will arraign thee first; for he who betrayed Him knows that He was condemned to death by the chief-priests and elders.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The thirty pieces of silver bear witness; Gethsemane bears witness, where the betrayal occurred; I speak not yet of the Mount of Olives, on which they were with Him at night, praying.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The moon in the night bears witness; the day bears witness, and the sun which was darkened; for it endured not to look on the crime of the conspirators.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The fire will arraign thee, by which Peter stood and warmed himself; if thou deny the Cross, the eternal fire awaits thee.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: I speak hard words, that thou may not experience hard pains.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Remember the swords that came against Him in Gethsemane, that thou feel not the eternal sword.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The house of Caiaphas will arraign thee, shewing by its present desolation the power of Him who was erewhile judged there.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Yea, Caiaphas himself will rise up against thee in the day of judgment, the very servant will rise up against thee, who smote Jesus with the palm of his hand; they also who bound Him, and they who led Him away.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Even Herod shall rise up against thee; and Pilate; as if saying, Why deniest thou Him who was slandered before us by the Jews, and whom we knew to have done no wrong?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For I Pilate then washed my hands.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: The false witnesses shall rise up against thee, and the soldiers who arrayed Him in the purple robe, and set on Him the crown of thorns, and crucified Him in Golgotha, and cast lots for His coat.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Simon the Cyrenian will cry out upon thee, who bore the Cross after Jesus.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: From among the stars there will cry out upon thee, the darkened Sun; among the things upon earth, the Wine mingled with myrrh; among reeds, the Reed; among herbs, the Hyssop; among the things of the sea, the Sponge; among trees, the Wood of the Cross;—the soldiers, too, as I have said, who nailed Him, and cast lots for His vesture; the soldier who pierced His side with the spear; the women who then were present; the veil of the temple then rent asunder; the hall of Pilate, now laid waste by the power of Him who was then crucified; this holy Golgotha, which stands high above us, and shews itself to this day, and displays even yet how because of Christ the rocks were then riven; the sepulchre nigh at hand where He was laid; and the stone which was laid on the door, which lies to this day by the tomb; the Angels who were then present; the women who worshipped Him after His resurrection; Peter and John, who ran to the sepulchre; and Thomas, who thrust his hand into His side, and his fingers into the prints of the nails.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For it was for our sakes that he so carefully handled Him; and what thou, who wert not there present, wouldest have sought, he being present, by God’s Providence, did seek.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Thou hast Twelve Apostles, witnesses of the Cross; and the whole earth, and the world of men who believe on Him who hung thereon.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Let thy very presence here now persuade thee of the power of the Crucified.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: For who now brought thee to this assembly? what soldiers?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: With what bonds wast thou constrained?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: What sentence held thee fast here now?

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: Nay, it was the Trophy of salvation, the Cross of Jesus that brought you all together.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: It was this that enslaved the Persians, and tamed the Scythians; this that gave to the Egyptians, for cats and dogs and their manifold errors, the knowledge of God; this, that to this day heals diseases; that to this day drives away devils, and overthrows the juggleries of drugs and charms.

verses.book: The Catechetical Lectures of S. Cyril.
verses.text: This shall appear again with Jesus from heaven; for the trophy shall precede the king: that seeing Him whom they pierced, and knowing by the Cross Him who was dishonoured, the Jews may repent and mourn; (but they shall mourn tribe by tribe, for they shall repent, when there shall be no more time for repentance;) and that we may glory, exulting in the Cross, worshipping the Lord who was sent, and crucified for us, and worshipping also God His Father who sent Him, with the Holy Ghost: To whom be glory for ever and ever.
